turns.content: What brings you in today?
This burning pain in my stomach after meals. Right here (gestures to upper abdomen). Been going on for months.
When did this start?
Hard to say exactly. Maybe five or six months ago? It started off mild but it's gotten worse lately.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?
It's right in the middle, up here (points to upper abdomen). Like a burning, gnawing feeling. Worse after eating, especially if I've had something spicy or heavy.
Have you had any fever or chills?
No. No fever. No chills or anything like that. Just the burning.
What medications are you currently taking?
Just some Rolaids now and then. Doesn't do much though.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?
No. Well, I've been told my blood pressure's a bit high at a checkup a couple years back, but I'm not on anything for it.
Are you allergic to any medications?
I don't think so. I've taken ibuprofen a couple times for sore muscles, but it made my stomach worse so I stopped.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?
Yeah, like I said, I tried those antacids from the store. And someone told me to take some over-the-counter acid pills. Didn't help much, kind of made it worse actually.
Does anything make it better or worse?

Definitely worse after eating. Especially if it's something spicy or greasy - I'm a chef so I can't avoid all of it. Lying down after meals makes the burning come back up too. Sitting up or walking a bit sort of helps.
How has this been affecting your daily life?
It's making my job tough. I'm a chef, so I have to taste food all day. Can't exactly avoid eating at work. I've been snapping at my staff more too. And I'm getting worried I'll have to take time off.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
I smoke, about half a pack a day. And yeah, I drink. Usually a couple beers after work, sometimes more on weekends.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?
My father had something like this, I think. He always complained about heartburn and took some medication for it. Not sure exactly what it was though.
Have you experienced anything like this before?
Not like this. I've had heartburn before like everyone else, but this is different. This burning sticks around for hours and comes back every single day. I can't ignore it anymore.
I've explained quite a bit — does that make sense so far?
Yeah, I think so. You're saying it could be reflux or some kind of inflammation in my stomach. Might need to run some tests to be sure.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?
Physically, it's like a 6 or 7 after meals. Right now it's maybe a 3. Emotionally, I'm just tired of dealing with it. It's wearing me down.
Do you have any questions for me?
Yeah. Can I still work while we figure this out? And do you think this is something that's going to need a big change in my diet or my schedule? I can't just stop cooking, that's my job.
What do you think might be going on?
Probably an ulcer or something like that. My dad had one. And the burning after eating... I don't know, seems like it fits. I'm worried it might be more serious though.
Is there anything else you think I should know?

No, I think that's pretty much it. Except... I should mention I've been taking ibuprofen sometimes for my back. It does seem to make the burning worse, but I figured it couldn't hurt since it was over-the-counter.
